about.name: João Marcos de Aquino Gonçalves
about.bio: Information Security intern with hands-on experience in vulnerability analysis, CI/CD pipelines, and frameworks such as NIST CSF 2.0, SSDF, and OWASP. ISC2 CC and Linux Foundation certified. Focused on AppSec and DevSecOps, with a solid foundation in Linux, Docker, and SAST/DAST tooling.

jobs.description: Performed security assessments on web applications using Burp Suite, Nikto, and OWASP ZAP, identifying vulnerabilities aligned with the OWASP Top 10. Supported the implementation of GRC controls based on NIST CSF 2.0 and NIST SP 800-218 (SSDF) frameworks. Leveraged Cyberint and BlackKite for external threat monitoring and third-party risk analysis. Produced technical documentation on DevSecOps practices, including an overview of OWASP SAMM and SAST/DAST tool integration in CI/CD pipelines. Collaborated with development teams on GitHub Actions pipeline reviews, integrating Trivy and SonarQube for continuous scanning of Docker images and source code.

Promoted from Intern to Level 2 in under a year, taking on server administration and internal development responsibilities. Developed automation scripts in Python, Bash, and JavaScript that reduced recurring task execution time by 40%. Administered Linux servers (Nginx, Webmail, DNS). Managed web applications and internal services, ensuring consistent performance and client satisfaction.

education.institution: Pontifical Catholic University of Minas Gerais (PUC Minas)

certifications: ISC2: Certified in Cybersecurity (CC) — valid through Feb. 2029

Cisco (2024–2026): Introduction to Cybersecurity; Networking Basics; Networking Devices and Initial Configuration; Endpoint Security
PUC Minas: Award-Winning Academic Project — PUC Caronas (2024)
